items.x: Auto-updates no longer stall behind the quit dialog — when Sparkle relaunches to install an update, the "Quit DeepTime?" confirmation steps aside.
Double-click a Log row to edit the session.
The AI week line now regenerates hourly in the background (and at session boundaries), so the focus screen opens with it ready instead of visibly reloading it.
The July strip's today square stays uncolored until the goal is met, matching the popover heat maps.

Sessions no longer end during long meetings — while Google Meet, Zoom, or any app keeps the display awake, the idle timer defers for the whole call (the old 10-minute backstop cut hour-long meetings short).

Focus Readout on the focus screen — the quote's old spot now shows your day and week: a Today ledger («Project» durations — total), an AI-written week line (on-device Apple Intelligence, fact-checked against your real numbers, template fallback), and the month as a two-row heat strip with hover breakdowns per day.
Quotes moved to the big screen — on multi-display setups, secondary displays show the rotating quote poster-huge while the focus screen is up.
Color schemes — Settings → Colors offers four 12-color palettes (Boost · Jewel · Soft · Aviation) with compact previews; switching restyles every project to the matching hue slot.
Add a forgotten session — Settings → Log → "＋ Add session…" logs a past block (deep or FreeTime) with project, start, and end.
Quit confirmation — ⌘Q asks "Quit DeepTime?" (Return quits, Esc cancels, or "don't ask again"; re-enable in Settings → App Icon).
Goals with live previews — Settings → Goals renders the menu-bar pill and the popover's pace line, ringing exactly what each setting controls.
Pressing Escape on the focus screen now answers with a toast — "Nope.
The focus screen's countdown pills now appear only when hovering a project row.
The Settings window resizes freely again and gained min sizing.
Project colors in swatch pickers now follow the active color scheme everywhere.
Pick the menu bar pill style visually — Settings → Menu Bar now shows all three styles as live mockups; click one to use it.
The chip popover's Settings row shows and responds to ⌘,.
The chip opens on the first click — clicking it while another app was frontmost used to need a second click.
The last project row in Settings → Projects is no longer cut off.
